name: cache
detail: Configure a flow cache parameter for a flow monitor.
syntax: cache {entries number | timeout {active | inactive | update} seconds | type {normal | permanent}}
noForm: no cache {entries | timeout {active | inactive | update} | type}

params.name: entries number
params.description: Specifies the maximum number of entries in the flow monitor cache. The range is 16 to 1048576. The default is 16640 for each switch in the stack.

params.name: timeout
params.description: Specifies the flow timeout.

params.name: active
params.description: Specifies the active flow timeout.

params.name: inactive
params.description: Specifies the inactive flow timeout.

params.name: update
params.description: Specifies the update timeout for a permanent flow cache.

params.name: seconds
params.description: The timeout value in seconds. The range is 30 to 604800 (7 days) for a normal flow cache. For a permanent flow cache the range is 1 to 604800 (7 days).

params.name: type
params.description: Specifies the type of the flow cache.

params.name: normal
params.description: Configures a normal cache type. The entries in the flow cache will be aged out according to the timeout active seconds and timeout inactive seconds settings. This is the default cache type.

params.name: permanent
params.description: Configures a permanent cache type. This cache type disables flow removal from the flow cache.

usageSummary: Each flow monitor has a cache that it uses to store all the flows it monitors.  Each cache has various configurable elements, such as the time that a flow is allowed to remain in it.  When a flow times out, it is removed from the cache and sent to any exporters that are configured for the corresponding flow monitor.

name: clear flow exporter
detail: Clear the statistics for a Flexible Netflow flow exporter.

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the name of a flow exporter.

params.name: exporter-name
params.description: (Optional) Name of a flow exporter that was previously configured.

params.name: statistics
params.description: Clears the flow exporter statistics.

usageSummary: The clear flow exporter command removes all statistics from the flow exporter.  These statistics will not be exported and the data gathered in the cache will be lost.  You can view the flow exporter statistics by using the show flow exporter statistics privileged EXEC command.

name: clear flow monitor
detail: Clear a flow monitor cache or flow monitor statistics and to force the export of the data in the flow monitor cache.
syntax: clear flow monitor [name] monitor-name [{[cache] force-export | statistics}]

params.name: name
params.description: Specifies the name of a flow monitor.

params.name: monitor-name
params.description: Name of a flow monitor that was previously configured.

params.name: cache
params.description: (Optional) Clears the flow monitor cache information.

params.name: force-export
params.description: (Optional) Forces the export of the flow monitor cache statistics.

params.name: statistics
params.description: (Optional) Clears the flow monitor statistics.

usageSummary: The clear flow monitor cache command removes all entries from the flow monitor cache.  These entries will not be exported and the data gathered in the cache will be lost.

name: collect counter
detail: Configure the number of bytes or packets in a flow as a non-key field for a flow record.

params.name: bytes
params.description: Configures the number of bytes seen in a flow as a non-key field and enables collecting the total number of bytes from the flow.

params.name: packets
params.description: Configures the number of packets seen in a flow as a non-key field and enables collecting the total number of packets from the flow.

params.name: long
params.description: Enables collecting the total number of bytes or packets from the flow using a 64-bit counter. After collection the counter resets to 0. permanent Enables collecting the total number of bytes or packets from the flow using a 64-bit counter. After collection the counter does not reset.

usageSummary: Flow packets are exported after cache timeout interval.  After they are exported, the count restarts from 0 if the long keyword is specified.  If the permanent keyword is specified, the counter increments for each byte or packet seen in the flow.

name: collect flow sampler
detail: Configure the flow sampler ID as a non-key field and enable the collection of the ID of the sampler that is assigned to the flow monitor.
usageSummary: The NetFlow Lite collect commands are used to configure non-key fields for the flow monitor record and to enable capturing the values in the fields for the flow created with the record.  The values in non-key fields are added to flows to provide additional information about the traffic in the flows.  A change in the value of a non-key field does not create a new flow.

name: collect interface
detail: Configure the input interface name as a non-key field for a flow record.
usageSummary: The NetFlow Lite collect commands are used to configure non-key fields for the flow monitor record and to enable capturing the values in the fields for the flow created with the record.  The values in non-key fields are added to flows to provide additional information about the traffic in the flows.  A change in the value of a non-key field does not create a new flow.

name: collect timestamp sys-uptime
detail: Configure the system uptime of the first seen or last seen packet in a flow as a nonkey field for a flow record.

params.name: first
params.description: Configures the system uptime for the time the first packet was seen from the flows as a nonkey field and enables collecting time stamps based on the system uptime for the time the first packet was seen from the flows.

params.name: last
params.description: Configures the system uptime for the time the last packet was seen from the flows as a nonkey field and enables collecting time stamps based on the system uptime for the time the most recent packet was seen from the flows.

usageSummary: The NetFlow Lite collect commands are used to configure nonkey fields for the flow monitor record and to enable capturing the values in the fields for the flow created with the record.  The values in nonkey fields are added to flows to provide additional information about the traffic in the flows.  A change in the value of a nonkey field does not create a new flow.

name: collect transport tcp flags
detail: Configure one or more TCP flags as a non-key field for a flow record and enable the collecting of values from the flow.
syntax: collect transport tcp flags [{ack | cwr | ece | fin | psh | rst | syn | urg}]
noForm: no collect transport tcp flags [{ack | cwr | ece | fin | psh | rst | syn | urg}]

params.name: ack
params.description: (Optional) Configures the TCP acknowledgment flag as a non-key field.

params.name: cwr
params.description: (Optional) Configures the TCP congestion window reduced flag as a non-key field.

params.name: ece
params.description: (Optional) Configures the TCP Explicit Congestion Notification echo (ECE) flag as a non-key field.

params.name: fin
params.description: (Optional) Configures the TCP finish flag as a non-key field.

params.name: psh
params.description: (Optional) Configures the TCP push flag as a non-key field.

params.name: rst
params.description: (Optional) Configures the TCP reset flag as a non-key field.

params.name: syn
params.description: (Optional) Configures the TCP synchronize flag as a non-key field.

params.name: urg
params.description: (Optional) Configures the TCP urgent flag as a non-key field.

usageSummary: The values of the transport layer fields are taken from all packets in the flow.  You cannot specify which TCP flag to collect.  You can only specify to collect transport TCP flags.

name: datalink flow monitor
detail: Apply a NetFlow Lite flow monitor to an interface.
syntax: datalink flow monitor monitor-name sampler sampler-name input
noForm: no datalink flow monitor monitor-name sampler sampler-name input

params.name: monitor-name
params.description: Name of the flow monitor to apply to the interface.

params.name: sampler sampler-name
params.description: Enables the specified flow sampler for the flow monitor.

params.name: input
params.description: Monitors traffic that the switch receives on the interface.

usageSummary: Before you apply a flow monitor to an interface with the datalink flow monitor command, you must have already created the flow monitor using the flow monitor global configuration command and the flow sampler using the sampler global configuration command.  To enable a flow sampler for the flow monitor, you must have already created the sampler.

name: debug flow exporter
detail: Enable debugging output for Flexible Netflow flow exporters.
syntax: debug flow exporter [[name] exporter-name] [{error | event | packets number}]
noForm: no debug flow exporter [[name] exporter-name] [{error | event | packets number}]

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the name of a flow exporter.

params.name: exporter-name
params.description: (Optional) The name of a flow exporter that was previously configured.

params.name: error
params.description: (Optional) Enables debugging for flow exporter errors.

params.name: event
params.description: (Optional) Enables debugging for flow exporter events.

params.name: packets
params.description: (Optional) Enables packet-level debugging for flow exporters.

params.name: number
params.description: (Optional) The number of packets to debug for packet-level debugging of flow exporters. The range is 1 to 65535.

name: debug flow monitor
detail: Enable debugging output for Flexible NetFlow flow monitors.
syntax: debug flow monitor [{error | [name] monitor-name [{cache [error] | error | packets packets}]}]
noForm: no debug flow monitor [{error | [name] monitor-name [{cache [error] | error | packets packets}]}]

params.name: error
params.description: (Optional) Enables debugging for flow monitor errors for all flow monitors or for the specified flow monitor.

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the name of a flow monitor.

params.name: monitor-name
params.description: (Optional) Name of a flow monitor that was previously configured.

params.name: cache
params.description: (Optional) Enables debugging for the flow monitor cache.

params.name: cache error
params.description: (Optional) Enables debugging for flow monitor cache errors.

params.name: packets
params.description: (Optional) Enables packet-level debugging for flow monitors.

params.name: packets
params.description: (Optional) Number of packets to debug for packet-level debugging of flow monitors. The range is 1 to 65535.

name: debug sampler
detail: Enable debugging output for Flexible NetFlow samplers.
syntax: debug sampler [{detailed | error | [name] sampler-name [{detailed | error | sampling samples}]}]
noForm: no debug sampler [{detailed | error | [name] sampler-name [{detailed | error | sampling}]}]

params.name: detailed
params.description: (Optional) Enables detailed debugging for sampler elements.

params.name: error
params.description: (Optional) Enables debugging for sampler errors.

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the name of a sampler.

params.name: sampler-name
params.description: (Optional) Name of a sampler that was previously configured.

params.name: sampling samples
params.description: (Optional) Enables debugging for sampling and specifies the number of samples to debug.

name: description
detail: Configure a description for a flow monitor, flow exporter, or flow record.

params.name: description
params.description: Text string that describes the flow monitor, flow exporter, or flow record.

modes: The following command modes are supported:
usageSummary: To return this command to its default setting, use the no description or default description command in the appropriate configuration mode.  The following example configures a description for a flow monitor: Device(config)# flow monitor FLOW-MONITOR-1 Device(config-flow-monitor)# description Monitors traffic to 172. 16.

name: destination
detail: Configure an export destination for a flow exporter.

params.name: hostname
params.description: Hostname of the device to which you want to send the NetFlow information.

params.name: ip-address
params.description: IPv4 address of the workstation to which you want to send the NetFlow information.

usageSummary: Each flow exporter can have only one destination address or hostname.  When you configure a hostname instead of the IP address for the device, the hostname is resolved immediately and the IPv4 address is stored in the running configuration.  If the hostname-to-IP-address mapping that was used for the original Domain Name System (DNS) name resolution changes dynamically on the DNS server, the device does not detect this, and the exported data continues to be sent to the original IP address, resulting in a loss of data.

name: dscp
detail: Configure a differentiated services code point (DSCP) value for flow exporter datagrams.

params.name: dscp
params.description: DSCP to be used in the DSCP field in exported datagrams. The range is 0 to 63. The default is 0.

usageSummary: To return this command to its default setting, use the no dscp or default dscp flow exporter configuration command.  The following example sets 22 as the value of the DSCP field in exported datagrams: Device(config)# flow exporter FLOW-EXPORTER-1 Device(config-flow-exporter)# dscp 22 Related Topics flow exporter, on page 260 Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.

name: export-protocol netflow-v9
detail: Configure NetFlow Version 9 export as the export protocol for a NetFlow Lite exporter.
usageSummary: The device does not support NetFlow v5 export format, only NetFlow v9 export format is supported.  The following example configures NetFlow Version 9 export as the export protocol for a NetFlow exporter: Device(config)# flow exporter FLOW-EXPORTER-1 Device(config-flow-exporter)# export-protocol netflow-v9 Related Topics flow exporter, on page 260 Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.

name: exporter
detail: Add a flow exporter for a flow monitor.

params.name: exporter-name
params.description: Name of a flow exporter that was previously configured.

usageSummary: You must have already created a flow exporter by using the flow exporter command before you can apply the flow exporter to a flow monitor with the exporter command.  To return this command to its default settings, use the no exporter or default exporter flow monitor configuration command.

name: flow exporter
detail: Create a NetFlow Lite flow exporter, or to modify an existing NetFlow Lite flow exporter, and enter NetFlow Lite flow exporter configuration mode.

params.name: exporter-name
params.description: Name of the flow exporter that is being created or modified.

usageSummary: Flow exporters export the data in the flow monitor cache to a remote system, such as a server running NetFlow collector, for analysis and storage.  Flow exporters are created as separate entities in the configuration.  Flow exporters are assigned to flow monitors to provide data export capability for the flow monitors.

name: flow monitor
detail: Create a flow monitor, or to modify an existing flow monitor, and enter flow monitor configuration mode.

params.name: monitor-name
params.description: Name of the flow monitor that is being created or modified.

usageSummary: Flow monitors are the NetFlow Lite component that is applied to interfaces to perform network traffic monitoring.  Flow monitors consist of a flow record and a cache.  You add the record to the flow monitor after you create the flow monitor.

name: flow record
detail: Create a NetFlow Lite flow record, or to modify an existing NetFlow Lite flow record, and enter NetFlow Lite flow record configuration mode.

params.name: record-name
params.description: Name of the flow record that is being created or modified.

usageSummary: A flow record defines the keys that NetFlow Lite uses to identify packets in the flow, as well as other fields of interest that NetFlow Lite gathers for the flow.  You can define a flow record with any combination of keys and fields of interest.  The supports a rich set of keys.

name: ip flow monitor
detail: Enable a NetFlow Lite flow monitor for IPv4 traffic that the device is receiving.
syntax: ip flow monitor monitor-name sampler sampler-name input
noForm: no ip flow monitor monitor-name sampler sampler-name input

params.name: monitor-name
params.description: Name of the flow monitor to apply to the interface. sampler sampler-name Enables the specified flow sampler for the flow monitor.

params.name: input
params.description: Monitors IPv4 traffic that the device receives on the interface.

usageSummary: Before you can apply a flow monitor to an interface with the ip flow monitor command, you must have already created the flow monitor using the flow monitor global configuration command.  When you add a sampler to a flow monitor, only packets that are selected by the named sampler will be entered into the cache to form flows.  Each use of a sampler causes separate statistics to be stored for that usage.

name: ipv6 flow monitor
detail: Enable a flow monitor for IPv6 traffic that the device is receiving.
syntax: ipv6 flow monitor monitor-name sampler sampler-name input
noForm: no ipv6 flow monitor monitor-name sampler sampler-name input

params.name: monitor-name
params.description: Name of the flow monitor to apply to the interface. sampler sampler-name Enables the specified flow sampler for the flow monitor.

params.name: input
params.description: Monitors IPv6 traffic that the device receives on the interface.

usageSummary: Before you can apply a flow monitor to the interface with the ipv6 flow monitor command, you must have already created the flow monitor using the flow monitor global configuration command.  When you add a sampler to a flow monitor, only packets that are selected by the named sampler will be entered into the cache to form flows.  Each use of a sampler causes separate statistics to be stored for that usage.

name: match datalink ethertype
detail: Configure the EtherType of the packet as a key field for a flow record.
usageSummary: A flow record requires at least one key field before it can be used in a flow monitor.  The key fields distinguish flows, with each flow having a unique set of values for the key fields.  The key fields are defined using the match command.

name: match datalink mac
detail: Configure the use of MAC addresses as a key field for a flow record.
syntax: match datalink mac {destination address input | source address input}
noForm: no match datalink mac {destination address input | source address input}

params.name: destination address
params.description: Configures the use of the destination MAC address as a key field.

params.name: input
params.description: Specifies the MAC address of input packets.

params.name: source address
params.description: Configures the use of the source MAC address as a key field.

usageSummary: A flow record requires at least one key field before it can be used in a flow monitor.  The key fields distinguish flows, with each flow having a unique set of values for the key fields.  The key fields are defined using the match command.

name: match ipv4
detail: Configure one or more of the IPv4 fields as a key field for a flow record.
syntax: match ipv4 {destination address | protocol | source address | tos | version}
noForm: no match ipv4 {destination address | protocol | source address | tos | version}

params.name: destination address
params.description: Configures the IPv4 destination address as a key field. For more information see match ipv4 destination address, on page 268.

params.name: protocol
params.description: Configures the IPv4 protocol as a key field.

params.name: source address
params.description: Configures the IPv4 destination address as a key field. For more information see match ipv4 source address, on page 269.

params.name: tos
params.description: Configures the IPv4 ToS as a key field.

params.name: version
params.description: Configures the IP version from IPv4 header as a key field.

usageSummary: A flow record requires at least one key field before it can be used in a flow monitor.  The key fields distinguish flows, with each flow having a unique set of values for the key fields.  The key fields are defined using the match command.

name: match ipv4 destination address
detail: Configure the IPv4 destination address as a key field for a flow record.
usageSummary: A flow record requires at least one key field before it can be used in a flow monitor.  The key fields distinguish flows, with each flow having a unique set of values for the key fields.  The key fields are defined using the match command.

name: match ipv4 source address
detail: Configure the IPv4 source address as a key field for a flow record.
usageSummary: A flow record requires at least one key field before it can be used in a flow monitor.  The key fields distinguish flows, with each flow having a unique set of values for the key fields.  The key fields are defined using the match command.

name: match ipv6
detail: Configure one or more of the IPv6 fields as a key field for a flow record.
syntax: match ipv6 {destination address | flow-label | protocol | source address}
noForm: no match ipv6 {destination address | flow-label | protocol | source address}

params.name: destination address
params.description: Configures the IPv4 destination address as a key field. For more information see match ipv6 destination address, on page 271.

params.name: flow-label
params.description: Configures the IPv6 flow-label as a key field.

params.name: protocol
params.description: Configures the IPv6 protocol as a key field.

params.name: source address
params.description: Configures the IPv4 destination address as a key field. For more information see match ipv6 source address, on page 272.

usageSummary: A flow record requires at least one key field before it can be used in a flow monitor.  The key fields distinguish flows, with each flow having a unique set of values for the key fields.  The key fields are defined using the match command.

name: match ipv6 destination address
detail: Configure the IPv6 destination address as a key field for a flow record.
usageSummary: A flow record requires at least one key field before it can be used in a flow monitor.  The key fields distinguish flows, with each flow having a unique set of values for the key fields.  The key fields are defined using the match command.

name: match ipv6 source address
detail: Configure the IPv6 source address as a key field for a flow record.
usageSummary: A flow record requires at least one key field before it can be used in a flow monitor.  The key fields distinguish flows, with each flow having a unique set of values for the key fields.  The key fields are defined using the match command.

name: match transport
detail: Configure one or more of the transport fields as a key field for a flow record.

params.name: source-port
params.description: Configures the transport source port as a key field.

usageSummary: A flow record requires at least one key field before it can be used in a flow monitor.  The key fields distinguish flows, with each flow having a unique set of values for the key fields.  The key fields are defined using the match command.

name: mode
detail: Specify the type of sampling and the packet interval for a NetFlow Lite sampler.

params.name: deterministic
params.description: Enables deterministic mode sampling for the sampler.

params.name: random
params.description: Enables random mode sampling for the sampler. 1 out-of window-size Specifies the window size from which to select packets. The range is 32 to 1022.

usageSummary: A total of four unique samplers (random or deterministic) are supported on the .  In deterministic mode, packets are chosen periodically based on the configured interval.  This mode has less overhead than random mode and can be useful when the device samples traffic that is random in nature.

name: option
detail: Configure optional data parameters for a flow exporter for NetFlow Lite.

params.name: exporter-stats
params.description: Configures the exporter statistics option for flow exporters.

params.name: interface-table
params.description: Configures the interface table option for flow exporters.

params.name: sampler-table
params.description: Configures the export sampler table option for flow exporters.

params.name: timeout seconds
params.description: (Optional) Configures the option resend time in seconds for flow exporters. The range is 1 to 86400. The default is 600.

usageSummary: The option exporter-stats command causes the periodic sending of the exporter statistics, including the number of records, bytes, and packets sent.  This command allows the collector to estimate packet loss for the export records it receives.  The optional timeout alters the frequency at which the reports are sent.

name: record
detail: Add a flow record for a NetFlow Lite flow monitor.

params.name: record-name
params.description: Name of a user-defined flow record that was previously configured.

usageSummary: Each flow monitor requires a record to define the contents and layout of its cache entries.  The flow monitor can use one of the wide range of predefined record formats, or advanced users may create their own record formats.

name: sampler
detail: Create a NetFlow Lite flow sampler, or to modify an existing NetFlow Lite flow sampler, and to enter NetFlow Lite sampler configuration mode.

params.name: sampler-name
params.description: Name of the flow sampler that is being created or modified.

usageSummary: Flow samplers are used to reduce the load placed by NetFlow Lite on the networking device to monitor traffic by limiting the number of packets that are analyzed.  You configure a rate of sampling that is 1 out of a range of 32 to 1022 packets.  Flow samplers are applied to interfaces in conjunction with a flow monitor to implement sampled NetFlow Lite.

name: show flow exporter
detail: Display flow exporter status and statistics.
syntax: show flow exporter [{export-ids netflow-v9 | [name] exporter-name [{statistics | templates}] | statistics | templates}]

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the name of a flow exporter.

params.name: exporter-name
params.description: (Optional) Name of a flow exporter that was previously configured.

params.name: statistics
params.description: (Optional) Displays statistics for all flow exporters or for the specified flow exporter.

params.name: templates
params.description: (Optional) Displays template information for all flow exporters or for the specified flow exporter.

name: show flow interface
detail: Display the NetFlow Lite configuration and status for an interface.

params.name: type
params.description: (Optional) The type of interface on which you want to display NetFlow Lite accounting configuration information.

params.name: number
params.description: (Optional) The number of the interface on which you want to display NetFlow Lite accounting configuration information.

name: show flow monitor
detail: Display the status and statistics for a NetFlow Lite flow monitor.
syntax: show flow monitor [[name] monitor-name [cache [format {csv | record | table}]] [statistics]]

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the name of a flow monitor.

params.name: monitor-name
params.description: (Optional) Name of a flow monitor that was previously configured.

params.name: cache
params.description: (Optional) Displays the contents of the cache for the flow monitor.

params.name: format
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the use of one of the format options for formatting the display output.

params.name: csv
params.description: (Optional) Displays the flow monitor cache contents in comma-separated variables (CSV) format.

params.name: record
params.description: (Optional) Displays the flow monitor cache contents in record format.

params.name: table
params.description: (Optional) Displays the flow monitor cache contents in table format.

params.name: statistics
params.description: (Optional) Displays the statistics for the flow monitor.

usageSummary: The cache keyword uses the record format by default.  The uppercase field names in the display output of the show flowmonitor monitor-name cache command are key fields that NetFlow Lite uses to differentiate flows.  The lowercase field names in the display output of the show flow monitor monitor-name cache command are nonkey fields from which NetFlow Lite collects values as additional data for the cache.

name: show flow record
detail: Display the status and statistics for a NetFlow Lite flow record.

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the name of a flow record. record-name (Optional) Name of a user-defined flow record that was previously configured.

name: show sampler
detail: Display the status and statistics for a NetFlow Lite sampler.

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the name of a sampler. sampler-name (Optional) Name of a sampler that was previously configured.

name: source
detail: Configure the source IP address interface for all of the packets sent by a NetFlow Lite flow exporter.

params.name: interface-type
params.description: Type of interface whose IP address you want to use for the source IP address of the packets sent by a NetFlow Lite flow exporter.

params.name: interface-number
params.description: Interface number whose IP address you want to use for the source IP address of the packets sent by a NetFlow Lite flow exporter.

usageSummary: The benefits of using a consistent IP source address for the datagrams that NetFlow Lite sends include the following: • The source IP address of the datagrams exported by NetFlow Lite is used by the destination system to determine from which device the NetFlow Lite data is arriving.  If your network has two or more paths that can be used to send NetFlow Lite datagrams from the device to the destination system and you do not specify the source interface from which the source IP address is to be obtained, the device uses the IP address of the interface over which the datagram is transmitted as the source IP address of the datagram.  In this situation the destination system might receive NetFlow Lite datagrams from the same device, but with different source IP addresses.

name: statistics packet protocol
detail: Collect protocol distribution statistics for a flow monitor.
usageSummary: Before you can collect protocol distribution statistics for a flow monitor with the statistics packet protocol command, you must define the protocol, source and destination ports, first and last time stamps and packet and bytes counters in the flow record.  If you do not define these fields, you will get the following warning: Warning: Cannot set protocol distribution with this Flow Record.  Require protocol, source and destination ports, first and last timestamps and packet and bytes counters.

name: template data timeout
detail: Specify a timeout period for resending flow exporter template data.

params.name: seconds
params.description: Timeout value in seconds. The range is 1 to 86400. The default is 600.

usageSummary: Flow exporter template data describes the exported data records.  Data records cannot be decoded without the corresponding template.  The template data timeout command controls how often those templates are exported.

name: transport
detail: Configure the transport protocol for a flow exporter for NetFlow Lite.

params.name: udp udp-port
params.description: Specifies User Datagram Protocol (UDP) as the transport protocol and the UDP port number.

usageSummary: To return this command to its default settings, use the no transport or default transport flow exporter configuration command.  The following example configures UDP as the transport protocol and a UDP port number of 250: Device(config)# flow exporter FLOW-EXPORTER-1 Device(config-flow-exporter)# transport udp 250 Related Topics flow exporter, on page 260 Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.

name: ttl
detail: Configure the time-to-live (TTL) value.

params.name: ttl
params.description: Time-to-live (TTL) value for exported datagrams. The range is 1 to 255. The default is 255.

usageSummary: To return this command to its default settings, use the no ttl or default ttl flow exporter configuration command.  The following example specifies a TTL of 15: Device(config)# flow exporter FLOW-EXPORTER-1 Device(config-flow-exporter)# ttl 15 Related Topics flow exporter, on page 260 Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15. 2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 298 PA R T V Network Management • Network Management , on page 301 Network Management • monitor session, on page 302 • monitor session destination, on page 304 • monitor session filter, on page 308 • monitor session source, on page 310 • show monitor, on page 313 • snmp-server enable traps, on page 316 • snmp-server enable traps bridge, on page 319 • snmp-server enable traps cpu, on page 320 • snmp-server enable traps envmon, on page 321 • snmp-server enable traps errdisable, on page 322 • snmp-server enable traps flash, on page 323 • snmp-server enable traps mac-notification, on page 324 • snmp-server enable traps port-security, on page 325 • snmp-server enable traps rtr, on page 326 • snmp-server enable traps snmp, on page 328 • snmp-server enable traps storm-control, on page 329 • snmp-server enable traps stpx, on page 330 Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.
